classification.loc_class: QC - Physics: waves, acoustics, and fluid dynamics

theory.canonical_statement: ID3440 records Because Most Irrational Relation as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `D:\00_Engine\CSV\all-edit_equation_arc_full.csv` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: $$ Because $\Phi$ is the "most irrational" number, it creates a rotational step size that minimizes the occurrence of low-order resonances. 3. The Fibonacci Lattice Algorithm As defined in [ID 1122] , the mapping for the $k$-th node (where $k$ runs from 0 to $N=153,599$) is: Z-Coordinate (Height): We slice the sphere into equal-area disks along the Z-axis. $$
canonical_equation.source_section_latex: $$ Because $\Phi$ is the "most irrational" number, it creates a rotational step size that minimizes the occurrence of low-order resonances. 3. The Fibonacci Lattice Algorithm As defined in [ID 1122] , the mapping for the $k$-th node (where $k$ runs from 0 to $N=153,599$) is: Z-Coordinate (Height): We slice the sphere into equal-area disks along the Z-axis. $$

wolfram_form.existing_from_tex: $$ Because $\Phi$ is the "most irrational" number, it creates a rotational step size that minimizes the occurrence of low-order resonances. 3. The Fibonacci Lattice Algorithm As defined in [ID 1122] , the mapping for the $k$-th node (where $k$ runs from 0 to $N=153,599$) is: Z-Coordinate (Height): We slice the sphere into equal-area disks along the Z-axis. $$
